page: Vultr vbm-256c-3072gb-8-mi355x-gpu is a bare metal server configured with dedicated AMD EPYC 9575F processors. The system features 256 physical cores and 256 vCPUs based on the x86_64 architecture, operating at a base clock speed of 3.3 GHz with a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio.

This server integrates eight AMD MI355X GPUs from the CDNA4 family, delivering a combined total of 2304 GB of VRAM. The system memory configuration includes 3072.0 GB of RAM, which translates to 12.0 GB of memory allocated per CPU core.
Local storage consists of 28608 GB of NVMe SSD capacity, providing high-density local storage for large datasets. The server also includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Given the high density of AMD CDNA4 GPUs and massive system memory, this bare metal instance is designed for large-scale artificial intelligence workloads, deep learning training, and complex data science jobs. The bare metal architecture eliminates virtualization overhead, offering high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for massive parallel processing tasks.
description: Vultr vbm-256c-3072gb-8-mi355x-gpu is a high-density bare metal server designed for intensive computational workloads. It features 256 dedicated physical cores powered by AMD EPYC 9575F processors running at 3.3 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration includes 3072.0 GB of system memory, providing 12.0 GB of RAM per core. For acceleration, the server is equipped with eight AMD MI355X GPUs from the CDNA4 family, offering a combined total of 2304 GB of VRAM. Local storage is provided by 28608 GB of NVMe SSD capacity, and the system includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. This bare metal server is optimized for large-scale artificial intelligence training, deep learning, and data science workloads requiring massive parallel processing capabilities and high-density memory resources without virtualization overhead.
